threat_model.name: Healthcare SaaS STRIDE + MITRE Analysis
threat_model.methodology: STRIDE + MITRE ATT&CK for Enterprise + MITRE ATT&CK for ICS (partial)

trust_boundaries.name: Public Internet
trust_boundaries.description: Untrusted external zone — patient browser, EHR partner APIs

trust_boundaries.name: Azure App Tier
trust_boundaries.description: FHIR API server, HL7 integration, patient portal API — AKS cluster

trust_boundaries.name: Azure Data Tier
trust_boundaries.description: Azure SQL, Cosmos DB, Blob Storage, Key Vault, Redis — private endpoints

trust_boundaries.name: External Healthcare Partners
trust_boundaries.description: Hospital EHR systems, lab networks, e-prescribing (Surescripts)

threats.description: Attacker uses stolen Azure AD B2C patient credentials (from credential stuffing or phishing) to authenticate to the FHIR API and query patient records. Token replay attack using intercepted JWT Bearer tokens from insecure transport.
mitigations: SMART-on-FHIR OAuth 2.0 with 15-minute token expiry
FIDO2 passkey authentication for patient portal
Azure AD Conditional Access requiring MFA + compliant device
FHIR Resource-level access control (patient/* vs user/*)
Anomaly detection on FHIR query patterns

threats.description: Insider threat (rogue clinician or compromised application account) modifies patient medication records, diagnoses, or allergy information. Could result in incorrect medical treatment — life safety impact beyond data breach.
mitigations: SQL Server Change Data Capture for all PHI tables
Row-level security enforcing clinician-patient relationship
Immutable audit log for all UPDATE/DELETE operations
Four-eyes approval for medication record modifications
Azure SQL Threat Detection for anomalous DML

threats.component: FHIR API Server + HL7 Integration Engine
threats.description: Healthcare provider disputes accessing patient record or transmitting PHI to unauthorized recipient. Without cryptographic audit evidence, HIPAA breach investigations cannot conclusively attribute actions to specific clinicians. HL7 message transmissions to partner EHRs have no non-repudiation mechanism.
mitigations: RSA-SHA256 signed FHIR audit events (AuditEvent resource)
Azure Immutable Storage for audit logs (write-once)
Application Insights telemetry correlated with FHIR AuditEvent
HL7 v2 message signing with digital envelope (S/MIME)
Non-repudiable MFA event linked to each PHI access

threats.description: PHI documents (diagnostic reports, discharge summaries, prescriptions) exposed via misconfigured public Blob container or predictable blob URL enumeration. Attacker can scrape entire patient document store without authentication.
mitigations: Set AllowBlobPublicAccess=false at storage account level
Use Azure Managed Identity for service-to-blob access (no keys)
Generate short-lived (15-minute) SAS tokens for patient downloads
Azure Private Endpoint for blob storage (no public internet path)
Defender for Storage with malware scanning and anomaly detection

threats.description: Container breakout from FHIR API pod to underlying AKS node, enabling access to all co-located pod secrets and mounted PHI data. Possible via privileged container, host path mount, or kernel exploit. Without Azure Policy OPA enforcement, any developer can deploy a privileged pod.
mitigations: Azure Policy: deny privileged containers, host path mounts, host network
Gatekeeper OPA enforce runAsNonRoot + readOnlyRootFilesystem
Istio mTLS between all pod-to-pod communications
AKS node pool isolation (system vs user node pools)
Regular kernel patching via AKS automatic node upgrades

threats.description: DDoS attack against FHIR API using HTTP/2 rapid reset (CVE-2023-44487) or FHIR-specific expensive queries (complex _search with chained parameters). In a clinical setting, DoS against the FHIR server denies emergency department clinicians access to medication and allergy information — patient safety impact.
mitigations: Azure Front Door DDoS protection (Standard tier)
Azure API Management: FHIR search query complexity limits
FHIR paging enforcement (max 100 results per query)
Rate limiting: 60 FHIR requests per minute per authenticated user
Emergency access fallback: cached read-only patient summary

threats.description: Accidental or malicious deletion of Azure Key Vault keys encrypting PHI column-level encryption in Azure SQL and PHI documents in Blob Storage. Without soft delete and purge protection, permanent deletion renders all encrypted PHI unrecoverable — HIPAA availability violation and irreversible data loss.
mitigations: Enable Key Vault soft delete (90-day retention)
Enable Key Vault purge protection (prevent permanent deletion)
RBAC: only key management service account can delete keys (not app identities)
Azure Monitor alerts on key deletion events
Key backup exported to secondary Key Vault in DR region

threats.description: The HL7 v2 MLLP listener accepts unauthenticated TCP connections on port 2575 from hospital ADT feeds. A malicious actor on the hospital network (or via a compromised hospital system) can inject crafted HL7 v2 ADT messages with oversized MSH segments or malformed data that triggers buffer overflow or code execution in the HL7 parser.
mitigations: HL7 MLLP TLS 1.3 + mutual certificate authentication
IP allowlist restricting MLLP connections to hospital IP ranges
HL7 message size limits (10MB max) enforced at network layer
Hardened HL7 parser with input validation (reject MSH > 1000 chars)
Containerized HL7 parser with no host access in case of exploit

threats.description: Session hijacking via XSS in patient portal Angular app. Attacker injects script via FHIR-sourced patient name field rendered in Angular template using DomSanitizer.bypassSecurityTrustHtml() — steals session cookie and authenticates as patient, accessing their health records.
mitigations: Remove all bypassSecurityTrustHtml() calls — use Angular safe binding
Content Security Policy (CSP) with nonce-based script-src
HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Strict session cookie flags
Angular strict mode with security lint rules
Subresource Integrity (SRI) for all third-party scripts

threats.description: Insider threat: nurse or administrative staff with legitimate PHI access bulk-exports patient data for personal use or sale. Without data loss prevention controls, large FHIR Bundle exports or repeated _search queries across all patients go undetected.
mitigations: FHIR minimum-necessary scopes (no patient/*.read for non-clinicians)
Alert on: >100 patient records accessed per hour by single user
Break-glass access tracking with mandatory reason code
FHIR AuditEvent resource generated for every PHI access
Microsoft Purview DLP policy on clinical data exports

attack_scenarios.name: PHI Ransomware via Supply Chain Compromise
attack_scenarios.description: Attacker compromises a NuGet package used in the FHIR server build pipeline, injecting backdoor that exfiltrates PHI before ransomware deployment. Targeting healthcare for 4x higher ransom probability.
mitigation_gaps: No network egress filtering from build pipeline

attack_scenarios.name: Rogue Clinician PHI Exfiltration
attack_scenarios.description: Disgruntled clinician uses legitimate FHIR API access to bulk-export patient records, then exfiltrates via personal OneDrive or email.
mitigation_gaps: No FHIR query volume anomaly detection
No data exfiltration DLP on clinical workstations
